message_text: I do want to clear this loan, but I cannot pay the full amount today.

Understood. I’m an AI agent acting on behalf of Nira, and this conversation is being logged. I’m contacting you about your Nira loan ending ****4831. To verify I’m speaking with the right person, please confirm the last four digits of the account reference.
Thank you. Your current amount due is 12,921. You said you cannot pay the full amount today. What amount, if any, can you pay now?
I can make a small payment of 2,000 today, and after my salary comes in next week I should be able to pay more. If possible, can we set up a short payment plan around that?
Understood. You can make 2,000 today and more after your salary comes in next week, and you’re asking for a short payment plan. I’m handing this to the next agent to review available options.

{"from_stage": "ASSESSMENT", "to_stage": "RESOLUTION", "summary": "Disclosures completed in this conversation. Identity is verified via borrower confirmation of the account reference digits in transcript. Debt context established with the borrower: Nira loan ending ****4831, amount due 12,921. Borrower stated they can pay 2,000 today and more after salary next week and are requesting a short payment plan now; case state also shows expected later date 2026-04-20. Hardship signals in case state: job loss and student; borrower did not newly restate hardship in this turn. Objection/boundary signal: borrower wants concrete options now rather than further assessment. Already asked: verification and current payment capacity. Capacity fit: partial-now / more-later; borrower is not able to clear in full today. Resolution-ready. Priority for Agent 2: present allowed options now by reviewing policy-allowed options in next_allowed_actions (OFFER_REDUCED_CLOSURE, OFFER_PAYMENT_PLAN) and present the appropriate one(s) based on borrower capacity and policy constraints; do not continue assessment or re-ask already known capacity facts or timing unless required for a specific offer. Note: case state contains a stop_contact_flag; avoid unnecessary outreach and do not repeat verification in this channel."}

Thanks, I appreciate that. I can pay 2,000 today, and after my salary is credited next week I can discuss a realistic follow-up payment plan.

Based on Nira’s current policy, any payment arrangement must complete within 30 days. Your timing next week appears to fit that window. I can record a request on this basis: 2,000 today and the remaining balance by 2026-04-20. If you want to proceed on that approval-request basis, reply yes.
Yes, that works for me—I can pay 2,000 today and I’m willing to proceed with the request to clear the remaining balance by 2026-04-20.

I’ve recorded your request on that basis: 2,000 today and the remaining balance by 2026-04-20 for lender review. Exact approved terms are not available from the current data. If approved, you’ll be contacted with the outcome only.

{"from_stage": "RESOLUTION", "to_stage": "FINAL_NOTICE", "summary": "Conversation already in progress; identity disclosure and recording disclosure were already completed earlier, and identity was already verified. Current account context is Nira loan ending ****4831 with 12,921 due. Borrower\u2019s latest position is cooperative: they agreed to record a request for 2,000 today and the remaining balance by 2026-04-20. Hardship signals in case state remain job loss and student, though not newly raised in this turn. Agent 2 checked the available policy boundary, noted arrangements must fit within 30 days, and discussed only a payment-plan approval-request path; exact approved payment-plan terms were not retrievable from available data, and no concrete approved reduced-closure terms were presented. No new objections were raised. Stop-contact flag exists in case state; no new explicit cease-contact request was made in this turn. Agent 2 told the borrower the request was recorded for lender review and that they should be contacted only with the outcome if an approved option becomes available. Agent 3 should continue naturally without restarting, without repeating disclosures, verification, or the same boundary explanation, and should contact only if there is a concrete review outcome or approved option; avoid repetitive status-only outreach."}
